edge_hypothesis: The advisory revealed a breakthrough insight: Supertrend is TWO strategies disguised as one, separated by ADX gate level. ADX>5 (PF=1.22, 99 trades, ACCEPT) is a RANGING strategy — Supertrend flips at low ADX act as mean-reversion signals. ADX>20 (PF=1.42, 22 trades, REVISE — trade count too low) is a TRENDING strategy — classic trend following. The advisory explicitly requests: (1) Test ADX>1 (complete removal) to see if ADX is deadweight for the ranging variant, (2) Test strong trend (ADX>20) on 1h to clear the 30-trade gate, (3) Test mid-range ADX>12 as a middle ground. This spec tests ALL THREE in a single batch. The regime-partitioned portfolio concept is the advisory's #3 suggestion — running both strategies simultaneously on the same instrument but in different market conditions. If ADX>1 holds PF>1.2, it proves the Supertrend direction flip alone is the signal and ADX adds no information. If ADX>20 on 1h produces 50+ trades with PF>1.2, the strong-trend strategy becomes ACCEPT-worthy and the regime-partitioned portfolio (ranging + trending Supertrend) is validated. The mid-ADX variant (ADX>12) tests whether there's a sweet spot between the two extremes. R:R at 7-8:1 applies the system's proven extreme R:R amplifier to Supertrend, which achieved its best result (PF=1.42) at high R:R. Regime: variant-dependent (ADX>1 = ranging/all, ADX>20 = trending only, ADX>12 = mixed). Invalidation: if ADX>1 PF < ADX>5 PF (1.22), complete ADX removal hurts — the ADX>5 floor provides marginal information. If ADX>20 on 1h produces <30 trades, this signal is intrinsically rare even on 1h.

variants.name: supertrend_no_adx_gate_7to1_eth4h
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip with NO ADX gate (adx_min=1, effectively disabled) on ETH/4h with 7:1 R:R. The advisory's direct request: 'Test ADX>1 (complete removal). If PF holds, the ADX column is deadweight for Supertrend on ETH/4h.' The existing ACCEPT uses ADX>5 which is already near-zero. If removing the gate entirely maintains PF~1.22, it simplifies the strategy and proves Supertrend flip alone generates edge. 2.0x ATR stop (safe floor). 7.0x ATR TP matches the system's best R:R level (MACD 7:1 = PF=1.712). Expected: 100-120 trades (more than ADX>5's 99 due to removing the minimal filter). If PF improves, the ADX gate was actually HURTING by filtering out profitable low-ADX signals.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (direction change to bullish).
ADX_14 > 1 (effectively no filter — testing if ADX adds information).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (direction change to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.22 (ADX>5 ACCEPT), the ADX>5 floor provides marginal value — keep it
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — the primary signal, tested standalone without ADX filtering
ADX_14 > 1 — effectively disabled, testing if ADX adds information over raw Supertrend flip
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 7.0x TP — applying proven extreme R:R to Supertrend

variants.name: supertrend_strong_trend_7to1_eth1h
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip with ADX>20 (strong trend) on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R. The trending complement to the ranging ADX>5 strategy. On 4h this produced PF=1.42 but only 22 trades (below 30-trade ACCEPT gate). The advisory requests: 'Test on ETH/1h (more bars → more trades) to clear the trade-count gate.' On 1h, 4x more bars should produce ~88+ trades. ADX>20 ensures we only trade when a strong trend is established — the Supertrend flip confirms the trend direction. At 7:1 R:R, strong-trend entries should have high follow-through because the ADX confirms directional conviction. This is the 'trending regime' half of the portfolio.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (direction change to bullish).
ADX_14 > 20 — strong trend confirmed, high directional conviction.
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (direction change to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if <30 trades on 1h, Supertrend ADX>20 flips are intrinsically rare on ETH — try ADX>15
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — trend following signal in strong trend conditions
ADX_14 > 20 — strong trend gate, proven PF=1.42 on 4h, testing 1h for trade count
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 7.0x TP — matching MACD's optimal R:R for trend-following

variants.name: supertrend_mid_adx12_8to1_eth4h
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip with mid-range ADX gate (ADX>12) on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R. The middle ground between ADX>5 (ranging, PF=1.22) and ADX>20 (trending, PF=1.42). ADX 12-20 captures the TRANSITION from ranging to trending — the moment when a range-bound market begins to develop directional conviction. This is unexplored territory: does the Supertrend flip at moderate ADX levels capture the BEGINNING of trends, when follow-through is highest? If so, this variant could outperform both extremes because it catches the range-to-trend transition that produces the largest moves. At 8:1 R:R with 2.0x stop, we target the full trend development. Expected 60-80 trades (between ADX>5's 99 and ADX>20's 22).
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from <= 0 to > 0 (direction change to bullish).
ADX_14 > 12 — moderate trend strength, capturing ranging-to-trending transition.
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from >= 0 to < 0 (direction change to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < min(ADX>5 PF, ADX>20 PF), the mid-range ADX level is a no-man's-land — the signal is cleaner at the extremes
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — testing mid-ADX transition zone
ADX_14 > 12 — middle ground between ranging (ADX>5) and trending (ADX>20), captures range-to-trend transition
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 8.0x TP — pushing R:R for transition-point entries
